mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises by over 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a broad-based demand contraction that depresses corporate earnings forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or positive returns dominate in >60% of co-declining months.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly contractions in the index of industrial production signal immediate supply-side bottlenecks that force rapid equity repricing, producing negative S&P 500 returns within one week during economic expansions.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative when INDPRO drops below -0.5% during expansion phases, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 1.0 percent, liquidity constraints vanish and risk appetite expands, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns as investors rotate into equities.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns show no positive bias when FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0%, or the signal produces negative returns in >50% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment combined with falling total nonfarm payrolls confirms a structural labor market deterioration that reduces aggregate income and forces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The co-occurrence of rising UNRATE and falling PAYEMS fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with Rank IC consistently below 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate revenue expectations and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week as markets price in stronger earnings.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates, or the signal yields negative returns in >55% of high-growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid monthly expansion in industrial production signals capacity constraints that trigger anticipated rate hikes, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month during high-inflation regimes.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of months where INDPRO accelerates during high-inflation periods, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.
